Data Structures

struct  buffer_t
 Buffer containing memory blocks, timestamp and metadata. More...
 

Functions

static buffer_t * buffer_new (memories_t *memories, uint64_t timestamp, uint32_t nb, properties_t *metadata)
 Allocate a new buffer.
 
static void buffer_free (buffer_t *buffer)
 Free a buffer.
 
static uint8_t * buffer_get_data (const buffer_t *buffer, size_t index)
 Get a pointer to the valid data of a memory block by index.
 

Detailed Description

Author
IMD Software Team
Attention

Copyright (c) 2026 STMicroelectronics. All rights reserved.

This software is licensed under terms that can be found in the LICENSE file in the root directory of this software component. If no LICENSE file comes with this software, it is provided AS-IS.

Defines buffer_t, which groups one or more memory blocks together with a presentation timestamp, a sequence number and optional metadata.

A buffer can hold several memory blocks (planes), making it suitable for planar data formats where each colour component is stored in a separate contiguous region. Typical examples include:

  • YUV planar – one memory block per plane: one for the Y (luma) component and one or two for the U/V (chroma) components (e.g. I420, NV12, NV21).
  • RGB planar – one memory block per colour channel (R, G, B).
  • Interleaved / packed – all components are stored in a single memory block (e.g. YUYV, RGB888). Only the first memory block (index 0) is used.

Use buffer_get_data to retrieve a pointer to the valid data region of each plane by its zero-based index.

Function Documentation

◆ buffer_free()

static void buffer_free ( buffer_t * buffer)
inlinestatic

Only the buffer structure itself is freed; the memory blocks and metadata are left untouched.

Parameters
[in]bufferPointer to the buffer to free (may be NULL).

◆ buffer_get_data()

static uint8_t * buffer_get_data ( const buffer_t * buffer,
size_t index )
inlinestatic

Returns a pointer to memory->data + memory->offset for the memory block at position index in the buffer's memory list.

Parameters
[in]bufferPointer to the buffer.
[in]indexZero-based index of the memory block.
Returns
Pointer to the start of valid data, or NULL if buffer is NULL or index is out of range.

◆ buffer_new()

static buffer_t * buffer_new ( memories_t * memories,
uint64_t timestamp,
uint32_t nb,
properties_t * metadata )
inlinestatic
Parameters
[in]memoriesPointer to the memory block list.
[in]timestampPresentation timestamp in nanoseconds.
[in]nbSequence number.
[in]metadataOptional metadata properties (may be NULL).
Returns
Pointer to the newly created buffer, or NULL on failure.
